en.title: Power BI Executive Cockpit over Salesforce
en.tagline: My transition to the data side — Salesforce reports were enough for reps, not for the exec team. Built a proper semantic model on top.
en.problem: Native Salesforce dashboards covered the day-to-day for reps and managers, but the exec team needed cross-cut views (segment × region × channel × product) that the out-of-the-box reports could not serve cleanly.
data: Salesforce opportunities, accounts, products, users — via connector
Historic closed deals for trend analysis
Calendar and quota tables for time & target views
solution: Pulled Salesforce data through Power Query, shaped into a star schema — one fact (Opportunity) plus conformed Date, Account, Product, User dimensions.
Wrote DAX measures for the handful of KPIs the exec team actually asks about: pipeline coverage, win rate, slippage, avg cycle, quota attainment.
Designed a 2-layer cockpit: one executive summary page, one drill path per function — mapped to real decisions on the meeting agenda.
Set up row-level security so regional leads see only their territory; the exec view stays clean.

impact.label: KPI layer
impact.detail: exec numbers match the detail views

en.takeaway: The data model is the product. Once the star schema is right, the DAX is small and the dashboard is obvious.

it.title: Cockpit executive Power BI sopra Salesforce
it.tagline: La mia transizione sul lato data — i report Salesforce bastavano per i rep, non per l'exec team. Costruito un modello semantico serio sopra.
it.problem: Le dashboard native Salesforce coprivano il quotidiano di rep e manager, ma l'exec team aveva bisogno di viste trasversali (segmento × region × canale × prodotto) che i report out-of-the-box non servivano in modo pulito.
data: Opportunity, account, prodotti, utenti Salesforce — via connector
Deal chiusi storici per l'analisi di trend
Tabelle calendario e quota per viste tempo & target
solution: Estratti i dati Salesforce con Power Query e modellati in star schema — un fact (Opportunity) più le dimensioni conformate Date, Account, Product, User.
Scritte misure DAX solo per i pochi KPI che l'exec chiede davvero: pipeline coverage, win rate, slippage, cycle medio, raggiungimento quota.
Disegnato un cockpit a 2 livelli: una pagina executive summary, un drill path per funzione — mappati su decisioni reali dell'agenda.
Row-level security: i regional vedono solo il loro territorio, la vista exec resta pulita.

impact.label: Layer KPI
impact.detail: i numeri exec combaciano con i dettagli

it.takeaway: Il data model è il prodotto. Una volta che lo star schema è giusto, il DAX è poco e la dashboard diventa ovvia.
